acquisition method: the presence of a sulfenamide bond would induce a trypsin miscleavage at the Lys leading to the formation of a longer peptide. The theoretical mass of this peptide (after removing 2 protons due to the formation of a sulfenamide bond) was not observed at the MS1 and MS2 levels in any of these conditions.
alkylation reagent: 10 mM NEM was added to block available free thiols.
alkylation concentration: 10 mM NEM was added to block available free thiols.
cleavage agent: the presence of a sulfenamide bond would induce a trypsin miscleavage at the Lys leading to the formation of a longer peptide.
instrument: analyzed by an Orbitrap Fusion Lumos tribrid mass spectrometer.
ionization type: Peptides were ionized by an NSI source set at 2.1 kV.
reduction reagent: the protein was reduced with 20 mM DTT.
reduction concentration: the protein was reduced with 20 mM DTT.
